Branch implement/T121-b at commit 6df487496b5023841e9bedba1592015cbcf55540, based at main 74f38b7 (verify merge-base==74f38b7; recurring stale-worktree fault). Worktree: /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2dcdac38e5d8df7e.
Diff: `git -C /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia diff 74f38b7 6df487496b5023841e9bedba1592015cbcf55540`.
Worker summary: new test/e2e/restart_onesided_test.go (`-tags e2e`, TestOneSidedRestartRecovery) + netns.go registry gets port 9104. Two runs (restart-only-edge; restart-only-concentrator) saturate past window 2048, restart one side, assert session_established 0→1 within 30s, survivor rebaselines>=1 + ~0 dropSuspect delta, D37 first-handshake tracks first-path-up. Deferred privileged run (o3 + llm-ubuntu-0), NOT part of merge gate.

Independently verify: (1) go build/vet -tags e2e clean; test excluded from default `go test ./...` and SKIPS unprivileged (not fails); (2) port 9104 unique across ALL e2e files (fresh grep) + added to the netns.go registry consistently (this repo had a prior port-registry-drift defect D51/T137 — make sure 9104 doesn't collide and the registry stays complete); (3) read the test logic: does it genuinely reproduce D36 (saturate past resequencerWindow 2048, restart ONLY one side, survivor re-anchors the wrapped init via T119 without suspect-drop) and D37 (first-handshake tracks path-up, not the 5s WG retransmit)? Are the surviving-side metric endpoints scraped correctly per direction (run A survivor=concentrator in peer netns on uplink IP; run B survivor=edge on loopback)? Do the counter names match T118 (wanbond_reseq_rebaselines / drop_suspect)? (4) tun_persist across restart is correct; (5) runbook invocations are exact + both hosts. Run `cd /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2dcdac38e5d8df7e && nix develop -c sh -c 'go build ./... && go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... && go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...' && nix develop -c just lint && nix develop -c just test`. Split criticism[] vs questions[]; file genuine defects. Be adversarial about a test that would silently pass (vacuous assertions) or mis-scrape the wrong side's metrics.

- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.
- environment: Bubblewrap "yolo" sandbox escape workflow (exchange scripts for reaching $HOME, system paths, or files outside the sandbox) plus in-sandbox nix/direnv usage. TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.
- izumi: Best-practice playbook for the izumi Scala ecosystem — distage (DI), distage-framework (roles/RoleAppMain), distage-testkit (test harness with memoization), distage-framework-docker (test containers), logstage (structured logging), and BIO (bifunctor effect typeclasses). Covers idiomatic bindings, Activation/Axis switching, Lifecycle discipline, plugin layout, and how to apply the `dual-tests` pattern inside distage so one test body runs against both a dummy and a real-DB adapter. TRIGGER when: writing or reviewing Scala code that imports `izumi.distage.*`, `izumi.logstage.*`, `izumi.functional.bio.*`, `distage.*`, `logstage.*`; designing a new distage plugin or Role; wiring a testkit `SpecZIO`/`Spec2`/`Spec1`; configuring `ContainerDef`/`DockerSupportModule`; choosing between `StandardAxis.Repo` or `Scene` tags; or implementing the dual-tests pattern in a distage project. The canonical reference project is https://github.com/7mind/distage-example.
- resilient-ws-ui: Guidelines for building applications that maintain reliable WebSocket connections to a backend and surface connection health clearly to the user. Invoke ONLY when the user explicitly references this skill by name (e.g. "/resilient-ws-ui", "use the resilient-ws-ui skill"). Do NOT auto-invoke based on topic match.
- tass: Target/Actual State Separation (TASS) pattern for reliable state management in event-driven control systems. TRIGGER when: implementing device or entity state management, designing control systems, working with target vs actual state separation, or building event-driven systems with commanded/observed state.
- cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review: Implement-flow adversarial per-task review rubric + stdout-JSON contract, as a portable promptTemplate for non-Claude harnesses (pi/Codex). Symmetric to cq:plan-review. Judges ONE task's implementation, splits findings into autonomously-fixable criticism[], user-only questions[], and out-of-scope defects[], and emits the verdict as JSON on stdout. Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.
- cq:implement:advance: Advance an implement-flow run one full pass — pick DAG-ready tasks, implement each in an isolated worktree, adversarially review, autonomously fix criticism, register user questions, and merge back in dependency order.
- cq:implement:start: Start an implement-flow run — resolve the target milestones (default: all open ones), validate their task DAG, then hand off to the /cq:implement:advance loop.
- cq:investigate: Start an investigate-flow run — defect intake + bootstrap, then run the /cq:investigate:advance pass inline.
- cq:investigate:advance: Advance an investigate-flow run one research round — read defect state, form/extend the hypothesis tree, dispatch read-only explorers, validate every citation against source, adjudicate node status, and on a confirmed root cause file-and-defer the fix to plan-flow.
- cq:plan: Start a plan-flow goal — create the goal (from free text OR one-or-more idea-ids, one goal per idea), then hand off to the planner for the first clarifying questions.
- cq:plan-review: Shared adversarial plan-review rubric — judge an emitted plan for a goal against the fine-grained / sequenced / testable / grounded / complete rubric, classify findings into new_questions / criticism / defects, and emit a single fenced-json verdict on stdout. The CANONICAL rubric source; Claude's plan-reviewer agent and every non-Claude (Codex/Pi) reviewer judge the same way and emit the same JSON.
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up: Add new scope to an EXISTING plan-flow goal — append the follow-up request, re-open the goal, and hand to the planner for a fresh clarifying round.
- cq:planners: Session-only planner-set override — parse a natural-language planner instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_planners/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- cq:reviewers: Session-only reviewer-set override — parse a natural-language reviewer instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_reviewers/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

17	// T121 is the privileged netns e2e for the D36/D37 one-sided-restart recovery fix
18	// (goals:G7). It is the VALIDATION half of D36's confirmed root cause: after a peer
19	// restarts, its outer-seq resets to ~1, far below the stale-high release point the
20	// pre-restart saturating stream advanced the SURVIVING side's per-peer resequencer
21	// `next` to. Absent a trusted re-anchor, admit() drops those low-seq frames — including
22	// the wrapped WG init/response — as SUSPECT (reseq.go), so the tunnel stays down until a
23	// WG rescue timer fires (field-reported as a multi-minute outage). T119 wired the
24	// authenticated peer-restart epoch change (T116/T117) to the per-peer
25	// Resequencer.RebaselineToLow re-anchor in dispatchInbound, and T120 drives a forced WG
26	// handshake initiation off the first path-up edge (D37). This test proves both, in BOTH
27	// restart directions, and captures the counters + 0->1 timestamps for the D36 record.
28	//
29	// Two SEPARATE runs (each its own fresh topology — the fixture's fixed veth names forbid
30	// two live topologies, mirroring TestStandbyLivenessBidirectional):
31	//
32	//   - restart-only-edge (run A): the SURVIVOR is the concentrator. Saturate edge->conc so
33	//     the concentrator's resequencer `next` advances past resequencerWindow (2048), then
34	//     restart ONLY the edge process (live paths, no endpoint change; no failover).
35	//   - restart-only-concentrator (run B): the SURVIVOR is the edge. Saturate conc->edge
36	//     (iperf3 reverse) so the edge's resequencer `next` advances past the window, then
37	//     restart ONLY the concentrator process (no failover — the concentrator role runs no
38	//     failover controller at all).
39	//
40	// For each direction it asserts:
41	//
42	//	(1) wanbond_session_established on the RESTARTED side transitions 0->1 within
43	//	    r121ReconvergeBudget — well under WG's own rescue timers (the ~180s
44	//	    REJECT_AFTER_TIME keypair expiry / the field multi-minute outage), targeting the
45	//	    ~25s both-ends-fresh baseline. The true per-direction magnitude is LOGGED (static
46	//	    analysis predicts ~10s for the edge-restart direction).
47	//	(2) the SURVIVING side's per-peer resequencer counters show
48	//	    wanbond_resequencer_rebaselines_total delta >= 1 (the trusted restart re-anchor
49	//	    fired) AND ~0 wanbond_resequencer_dropped_suspect_frames_total delta (the wrapped
50	//	    init was re-anchored, NOT suspect-dropped — the D36 pathology would drop dozens to
51	//	    hundreds over the outage).
52	//	(3) D37: from cold start, the edge's time-to-first-handshake tracks the first path-up
53	//	    edge (+~1 RTT), NOT a 5s WG retransmit-timer multiple — asserted from the edge log
54	//	    timestamps at INITIAL bring-up (before any restart), in both runs.
55	//
56	// EXECUTION IS DEFERRED (G2 pattern). The netns tier needs the privileged two-namespace
57	// fixture + CAP_NET_ADMIN + /dev/net/tun and is NOT run in the unit environment; this file
58	// must COMPILE and vet under -tags e2e (`go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e` + `go build -tags e2e
59	// ./test/e2e`) and, run WITHOUT privileges, SKIPS (see requireNetAdmin) rather than fails.

63	// merge gate). SSH always with `-F none` (the workers' system ssh_config is broken); sudo
64	// resets PATH, so re-inject it for the test process (it shells out to go/ip/nsenter/iperf3).
65	// Sync the working tree first (tar-over-ssh, excluding .git .cq .codegraph .claude vendor
66	// result) and `apt-get install -y iperf3 gcc` + Go 1.26.4 to /usr/local/go once per host.
67	//
68	//	# aarch64 (Oracle Cloud):
69	//	ssh -F none -i /run/agenix/llm-ssh-key ubuntu@o3.7mind.io \
70	//	  'cd ~/wanbond && sudo env PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin \
71	//	     HOME=/root GOTOOLCHAIN=local GOCACHE=/tmp/gocache \
72	//	     go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run TestOneSidedRestartRecovery -v'
73	//
74	//	# amd64 (different network):
75	//	ssh -F none -i /run/agenix/llm-ssh-key ubuntu@llm-ubuntu-0.pgtr.7mind.io \
76	//	  'cd ~/wanbond && sudo env PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin \
77	//	     HOME=/root GOTOOLCHAIN=local GOCACHE=/tmp/gocache \
78	//	     go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run TestOneSidedRestartRecovery -v'
79	const (
80		// r121EdgeMetrics is the EDGE's /metrics listen (loopback — the edge runs in the base
81		// test-process netns, so 127.0.0.1 is directly scrapeable). The concentrator runs in the
82		// PEER netns where a 127.0.0.1 listener is unreachable from the base netns, so it binds
83		// its primary uplink address instead (r121ConcMetricsListen, reachable over the veth).
84		// Both use the SAME new port 9104 (distinct addresses/netns — no collision); 9104 is this
85		// file's registry entry (see netns.go metricsPortRegistry).
86		r121MetricsPort    = 9104
87		r121EdgeMetrics    = "127.0.0.1:9104"
88		r121EdgeMetricsURL = "http://" + r121EdgeMetrics + "/metrics"
89	
90		// r121ReconvergeBudget bounds one-sided-restart reconvergence (session_established
91		// 0->1). It sits WELL under WG's own rescue timers and targets the ~25s both-ends-fresh
92		// baseline, with headroom so a slow CI host does not flake. The measured per-direction
93		// magnitude is LOGGED for the D36 record (edge-restart predicted ~10s).
94		r121ReconvergeBudget = 30 * time.Second
95	
96		// r121D37Budget bounds cold-start time-to-first-handshake measured from the first
97		// path-up edge: it must track path-up (+~1 RTT), NOT a 5s WG retransmit-timer multiple
98		// (the D37 symptom). Strictly below one 5s retransmit so a regression to the retransmit
99		// path (>=5s) fails the assertion.
100		r121D37Budget = 4 * time.Second
101	
102		// r121SatSeconds is the saturating-flow duration: long enough to push the surviving
103		// side's resequencer `next` WELL past resequencerWindow (2048) frames — the D36
104		// precondition, without which the restarted peer's outer-seq-~1 frames would admit
105		// normally and the SUSPECT-drop pathology would never arise.
106		r121SatSeconds = 6
107	
108		// r121ReseqWindow mirrors internal/bind.resequencerWindow (2048, unexported): the
109		// precondition asserts the survivor released MORE than one window of frames, so its
110		// release point `next` is provably past the window before the restart.
111		r121ReseqWindow = 2048
112	
113		// r121DropSuspectSlack tolerates at most a few genuine stale-high reorder stragglers
114		// being SUSPECT-dropped around the restart (which T119's low-anchor re-anchor drops
115		// CORRECTLY). It is far below the dozens-to-hundreds the D36 pathology would drop while
116		// every wrapped-init retransmit is suspect-dropped over the multi-minute outage, so it
117		// still catches the defect while not flaking on bounded cross-path reorder.
118		r121DropSuspectSlack = 3
119	)
120	
121	// TestOneSidedRestartRecovery runs both D36/D37 restart directions (run A: restart only
122	// the edge; run B: restart only the concentrator), each on its own fresh topology.
123	func TestOneSidedRestartRecovery(t *testing.T) {
124		requireNetAdmin(t)
125		bin := buildWanbond(t)
126	
127		t.Run("restart-only-edge", func(t *testing.T) {
128			testRestartOnlyEdge(t, bin)
129		})
130		t.Run("restart-only-concentrator", func(t *testing.T) {
131			testRestartOnlyConcentrator(t, bin)
132		})
133	}
134	
135	// testRestartOnlyEdge is run A: the concentrator is the SURVIVOR. The edge saturates the
136	// bond (edge->conc), advancing the concentrator's per-peer resequencer `next` past the
137	// window; then ONLY the edge process restarts. The concentrator must re-anchor (rebaseline)
138	// on the edge's authenticated restart epoch and admit the wrapped init instead of
139	// suspect-dropping it, and the edge's session_established must return 0->1 within budget.
140	func testRestartOnlyEdge(t *testing.T, bin string) {
141		t.Helper()
142		top := SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
143		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink — the concentrator uplink the edge targets
144		survivorURL := "http://" + primary.concIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort) + "/metrics"
145	
146		edge, conc, edgeArgv, _ := r121BringUp(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, primary.concIP)
147	
148		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
149			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
150		}
151		// (1) initial convergence: the restarted side we will re-assert is the edge; confirm it
152		// established at all before the restart so the post-restart 0->1 is a genuine transition.
153		waitSessionEstablished(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
154		// (3) D37 cold-start first-handshake, from the edge log at initial bring-up.
155		assertD37FirstHandshake(t, edge)
156	
157		// D36 precondition: saturate edge->conc so the CONCENTRATOR resequencer `next` advances
158		// well past the window. iperf3 client on the edge, one-shot server in the peer netns.
159		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
160		time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
161		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(r121SatSeconds))
162		time.Sleep(time.Duration(r121SatSeconds+1) * time.Second) // let the flow run to completion + queues drain
163	
164		before := scrapeMetrics(t, survivorURL)
165		relBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqReleased, "concentrator survivor")
166		if relBefore <= r121ReseqWindow {
167			t.Fatalf("D36 precondition unmet: concentrator survivor released only %.0f frames (<= window %d) — `next` did not advance past the window, so a restart could not exercise the SUSPECT-drop path",
168				relBefore, r121ReseqWindow)
169		}
170		rebaseBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "concentrator survivor")
171		dropBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "concentrator survivor")
172	
173		// RESTART ONLY THE EDGE (tun_persist keeps wanbond0 + its inner addr across the stop).
174		restartAt := time.Now()
175		top.stopAndWait(t, edge)
176		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", edgeArgv...)
177		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
178			t.Fatalf("edge %s not re-adopted after restart\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
179		}
180	
181		// (1) the restarted edge's session_established returns 0->1 within budget.
182		waitSessionEstablished(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
183		reconv := time.Since(restartAt)
184		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, r121ReconvergeBudget) {
185			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not carry inner traffic within %s of the edge restart\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
186				r121ReconvergeBudget, edge.log(), conc.log())
187		}
188	
189		// (2) the SURVIVING concentrator re-anchored (rebaselines>=1) and did NOT suspect-drop
190		// the wrapped init (~0 dropSuspect delta).
191		after := scrapeMetrics(t, survivorURL)
192		rebaseAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "concentrator survivor")
193		dropAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "concentrator survivor")
194		r121AssertRecovery(t, "edge-restart", reconv, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, relBefore)
195	}
196	
197	// testRestartOnlyConcentrator is run B: the edge is the SURVIVOR. The concentrator
198	// saturates the bond in REVERSE (conc->edge via iperf3 -R), advancing the edge's per-peer
199	// resequencer `next` past the window; then ONLY the concentrator process restarts (no
200	// failover — the concentrator role runs none). The edge must re-anchor on the
201	// concentrator's authenticated restart epoch, and the concentrator's session_established
202	// must return 0->1 within budget.
203	func testRestartOnlyConcentrator(t *testing.T, bin string) {
204		t.Helper()
205		top := SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
206		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
207		restartedURL := "http://" + primary.concIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort) + "/metrics"
208	
209		edge, conc, _, concArgv := r121BringUp(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, primary.concIP)
210	
211		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
212			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
213		}
214		waitSessionEstablished(t, restartedURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
215		// (3) D37 cold-start first-handshake is an EDGE property; assert it on the edge log at
216		// initial bring-up (the concentrator is the responder and initiates nothing).
217		assertD37FirstHandshake(t, edge)
218	
219		// D36 precondition: saturate conc->edge (reverse) so the EDGE resequencer `next` advances
220		// past the window. The one-shot server runs in the peer netns; iperf3 -R makes the SERVER
221		// (concentrator side) send to the client (edge), so the edge is the receiver.
222		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
223		time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
224		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-R", "-t", strconv.Itoa(r121SatSeconds))
225		time.Sleep(time.Duration(r121SatSeconds+1) * time.Second)
226	
227		before := scrapeMetrics(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL)
228		relBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqReleased, "edge survivor")
229		if relBefore <= r121ReseqWindow {
230			t.Fatalf("D36 precondition unmet: edge survivor released only %.0f frames (<= window %d) — `next` did not advance past the window",
231				relBefore, r121ReseqWindow)
232		}
233		rebaseBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "edge survivor")
234		dropBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "edge survivor")
235	
236		// RESTART ONLY THE CONCENTRATOR (tun_persist keeps the peer-netns wanbond0 + concInner).
237		restartAt := time.Now()
238		top.stopAndWait(t, conc)
239		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", concArgv...)
240		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
241			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s not re-adopted after restart\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
242		}
243	
244		// (1) the restarted concentrator's session_established returns 0->1 within budget.
245		waitSessionEstablished(t, restartedURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
246		reconv := time.Since(restartAt)
247		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, r121ReconvergeBudget) {
248			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not carry inner traffic within %s of the concentrator restart\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
249				r121ReconvergeBudget, edge.log(), conc.log())
250		}
251	
252		// (2) the SURVIVING edge re-anchored and did NOT suspect-drop the wrapped init.
253		after := scrapeMetrics(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL)
254		rebaseAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "edge survivor")
255		dropAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "edge survivor")
256		r121AssertRecovery(t, "concentrator-restart", reconv, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, relBefore)
257	}
258	
259	// r121AssertRecovery is the shared (2) assertion + D36-record logging for both directions:
260	// the survivor rebaselined at least once and did not suspect-drop the wrapped init.
261	func r121AssertRecovery(t *testing.T, dir string, reconv time.Duration, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, released float64) {
262		t.Helper()
263		rebaseDelta := rebaseAfter - rebaseBefore
264		dropDelta := dropAfter - dropBefore
265		// Capture the counters + reconvergence magnitude in the test output for the D36 record.
266		t.Logf("D36/%s: session_established 0->1 in %s (budget %s); survivor released=%.0f (> window %d) "+
267			"rebaselines %.0f->%.0f (delta %.0f) dropSuspect %.0f->%.0f (delta %.0f)",
268			dir, reconv.Round(time.Millisecond), r121ReconvergeBudget, released, r121ReseqWindow,
269			rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, rebaseDelta, dropBefore, dropAfter, dropDelta)
270		if rebaseDelta < 1 {
271			t.Errorf("D36/%s: survivor rebaselines delta = %.0f, want >= 1 — the authenticated peer-restart epoch change did "+
272				"not re-anchor the survivor's resequencer, so the restarted peer's low-outer-seq wrapped init would be "+
273				"SUSPECT-dropped (the D36 multi-minute-outage pathology). T119 wires this via dispatchInbound's "+
274				"RebaselineToLow on epochChanged.", dir, rebaseDelta)
275		}
276		if dropDelta > r121DropSuspectSlack {
277			t.Errorf("D36/%s: survivor dropSuspect delta = %.0f across the restart, want ~0 (<= %d slack for stale-high "+
278				"reorder stragglers) — the wrapped WG init/response was SUSPECT-dropped rather than re-anchored, which is "+
279				"exactly the D36 defect: recovery then waits on a WG rescue timer instead of the trusted re-anchor.",
280				dir, dropDelta, r121DropSuspectSlack)
281		}
282	}
283	
284	// assertD37FirstHandshake is the (3) D37 assertion: from cold start, the edge's first WG
285	// handshake tracks the first path-up edge (+~1 RTT), NOT a 5s WG retransmit-timer multiple.
286	// It reads the edge log timestamps (INFO 'path liveness transition' to=up and 'session
287	// established'), the same evidence TestSessionEstablishedTransitions uses.
288	func assertD37FirstHandshake(t *testing.T, edge *proc) {
289		t.Helper()
290		logText := edge.log()
291		pathUpAt, ok := pathLivenessUpTime(logText)
292		if !ok {
293			t.Fatalf("D37: no 'path liveness transition' to=up record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
294		}
295		sessionAt, ok := logRecordTime(logText, "session established")
296		if !ok {
297			t.Fatalf("D37: no 'session established' record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
298		}
299		if sessionAt.Before(pathUpAt) {
300			t.Errorf("D37: session established (%s) preceded path up (%s); expected path up FIRST",
301				sessionAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), pathUpAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
302		}
303		delta := sessionAt.Sub(pathUpAt)
304		t.Logf("D37 cold start: first handshake %s after the first path-up edge (budget %s)", delta.Round(time.Millisecond), r121D37Budget)
305		if delta > r121D37Budget {
306			t.Errorf("D37: cold-start time-to-first-handshake = %s after path-up, want <= %s (~1 RTT) — a value tracking a 5s "+
307				"WG retransmit-timer multiple means the first init fired pre-liveness and was NOT re-driven off the path-up "+
308				"edge (the D37 symptom). T120 drives a forced SendHandshakeInitiation off the first path-up edge.",
309				delta, r121D37Budget)
310		}
311	}
312	
313	// r121BringUp starts the concentrator (peer netns, via nsenter) and the edge (base netns),
314	// waits for both wanbond0 TUNs, plumbs the inner overlay addresses, and returns both procs
315	// plus the exact argv each was started with (so a restart re-launches an identical process).
316	// Both ends run tun_persist=true so the inner address survives a full daemon stop/start, and
317	// at [log] level "info" so the D37 path-up / session-established records are emitted. The
318	// edge binds its /metrics on loopback; the concentrator binds its primary uplink address so
319	// the base netns can scrape it over the veth.
320	func r121BringUp(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, concMetricsIP string) (edge, conc *proc, edgeArgv, concArgv []string) {
321		t.Helper()
322	
323		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
324		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
325		psk := randKey(t)
326	
327		concMetricsListen := concMetricsIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort)
328	
329		dir := t.TempDir()
330		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"),
331			r121EdgeConfig(psk, edgePriv, concPub, paths, r121EdgeMetrics))
332		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"),
333			r121ConcConfig(psk, concPriv, edgePub, paths, concMetricsListen))
334	
335		edgeArgv = []string{bin, "--config", edgeCfg}
336		concArgv = []string{"nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg}
337	
338		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", concArgv...)
339		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", edgeArgv...)
340	
341		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
342			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
343		}
344		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
345			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
346		}
347		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
348		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
349		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
350		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
351		return edge, conc, edgeArgv, concArgv
352	}
353	
354	// r121EdgeConfig renders the edge TOML: one source-bound socket per path targeting that
355	// path's concentrator address, tun_persist so the inner addr survives a restart, an edge
356	// /metrics block, and info-level logging for the D37 records.
357	func r121EdgeConfig(psk, edgePriv, concPub string, paths []pathSpec, metricsListen string) string {
358		var b strings.Builder
359		for _, p := range paths {
360			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
361		}
362		return fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
363	psk = "%s"
364	tun_persist = true
365	
366	%s[metrics]
367	listen = %q
368	
369	[wireguard]
370	private_key = "%s"
371	
372	[[wireguard.peers]]
373	public_key = "%s"
374	endpoint = "%s:%d"
375	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
376	
377	[log]
378	level = "info"
379	`, psk, b.String(), metricsListen, edgePriv, concPub, paths[primaryPathIdx].concIP, listenPort, concInner)
380	}
381	
382	// r121ConcConfig renders the concentrator TOML: one source-bound socket per path, tun_persist,
383	// a /metrics block bound to the primary uplink address (base-netns-scrapeable), and info logs.
384	func r121ConcConfig(psk, concPriv, edgePub string, paths []pathSpec, metricsListen string) string {
385		var b strings.Builder
386		for _, p := range paths {
387			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
388		}
389		return fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
390	psk = "%s"
391	tun_persist = true
392	
393	%s[metrics]
394	listen = %q
395	
396	[wireguard]
397	private_key = "%s"
398	listen_port = %d
399	
400	[[wireguard.peers]]
401	public_key = "%s"
402	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
403	
404	[log]
405	level = "info"
406	`, psk, b.String(), metricsListen, concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner)
407	}
408	
409	// r121PeerCounter reads a per-peer resequencer counter for the sole (unnamed primary) peer.
410	// Both a single-peer edge and a single-peer concentrator expose their one peer UNNAMED
411	// (peer=""), per BoundPeerNames' primary override (see multipeer_test.go). Missing is treated
412	// as 0 for a counter (a rebaseline/drop that never happened yields no series until the first
413	// increment); the caller's deltas remain correct.
414	func r121PeerCounter(t *testing.T, exp metrics.Exposition, name, who string) float64 {
415		t.Helper()
416		v, ok := exp.PeerValue(name, "")
417		if !ok {
418			// released must be present once traffic flowed; its absence is a real fault, not a
419			// legitimately-zero counter, so surface it rather than silently reading 0.
420			if name == metrics.MetricReseqReleased {
421				t.Fatalf("%s{peer=\"\"} absent from the %s /metrics after traffic flowed", name, who)
422			}
423			return 0
424		}
425		return v
426	}
427	
428	// requireNetAdmin skips (does NOT fail) when the process cannot build the netns fixture for
429	// lack of CAP_NET_ADMIN — so `go test -tags e2e` in an unprivileged environment SKIPS this
430	// scenario rather than failing it. Under the privileged hardware suite (sudo / the TestMain
431	// user+net namespace) the probe succeeds and the test runs. It probes by creating and then
432	// deleting a throwaway dummy link.
433	func requireNetAdmin(t *testing.T) {
434		t.Helper()
435		const probe = "wbR121probe"
436		if out, err := exec.Command("ip", "link", "add", probe, "type", "dummy").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
437			t.Skipf("no CAP_NET_ADMIN to build the netns fixture (%v: %s) — the one-sided-restart e2e requires the privileged suite; skipping",
438				err, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)))
439		}
440		_ = exec.Command("ip", "link", "del", probe).Run()
441	}

1	//go:build e2e

3	package e2e

11	)
12	
13	// nsEnvMarker guards the one-shot re-exec below.
14	const nsEnvMarker = "WANBOND_E2E_NS"
15	
16	// TestMain re-execs the e2e test binary inside a FRESH network namespace so the
17	// fixtures never touch the host's networking. Under real root (the sudo target)
18	// it uses a plain mount+net namespace; unprivileged (the sandbox) it uses an
19	// unprivileged user+net namespace, which still grants CAP_NET_ADMIN for veth and
20	// netem. The PID-addressed peer namespace in netns.go needs no writable
21	// /run/netns, so this works in both environments.
22	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
23		if os.Getenv(nsEnvMarker) == "" {
24			self, err := os.Executable()
25			if err != nil {
26				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: cannot find test binary:", err)
27				os.Exit(1)
28			}
29			unshareArgs := []string{"-Urmn"}
30			if os.Geteuid() == 0 {
31				unshareArgs = []string{"-mn"}
32			}
33			args := append([]string{}, unshareArgs...)
34			args = append(args, "--", self)
35			args = append(args, os.Args[1:]...)
36	
37			cmd := exec.Command("unshare", args...)
38			cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), nsEnvMarker+"=1")
39			cmd.Stdin, cmd.Stdout, cmd.Stderr = os.Stdin, os.Stdout, os.Stderr
40			err = cmd.Run()
41			if err == nil {
42				os.Exit(0)
43			}
44			var ee *exec.ExitError
45			if errors.As(err, &ee) {
46				os.Exit(ee.ExitCode())
47			}
48			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "e2e: namespace re-exec failed:", err)
49			os.Exit(1)
50		}
51		os.Exit(m.Run())
52	}

All verification complete. The evidence is decisive on two points.

**Verified green:** `go build ./...`, `go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...`, `go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...` all clean in the worktree; `just lint` 0 issues; `just test` (default suite) green and excludes the e2e package via the `//go:build e2e` tag. Port 9104 verified unique by fresh grep across all of `test/e2e/` and the `netns.go` registry comment is updated consistently (no D51-style drift). Merge-base of `implement/T121-b` against `main` is exactly `74f38b7` — no stale worktree. Counter names use the `internal/metrics` constants (immune to name drift; they resolve to the real T118 names `wanbond_resequencer_rebaselines_total` / `wanbond_resequencer_dropped_suspect_frames_total`). The saturation topology is directionally correct (run A: iperf3 edge→conc so the concentrator's resequencer advances; run B: `iperf3 -R` so the edge's does), the `relBefore > 2048` precondition guards against a vacuous pass, `stopAndWait`/`startProc` restart discipline is sound, and the D37 log-timestamp assertion reuses the same evidence as `TestSessionEstablishedTransitions` with a 4s budget that discriminates against a 5s retransmit multiple.

**Finding 1 — the survivor's counters are never actually scraped (mis-scrape, /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2dcdac38e5d8df7e/test/e2e/restart_onesided_test.go:414-426).** `r121PeerCounter` reads `exp.PeerValue(name, "")`, which matches only a series carrying a `peer=""` **label**. But `metrics.NewCollector` sets `multiPeer := len(src.PeerNames()) > 1` (internal/metrics/metrics.go:260-266) and both daemons here bind exactly ONE peer, so the reseq series are emitted **unlabeled** — `PeerValue` returns `(0, false)` unconditionally. The repo's own `TestExpositionReseqRebaselineAndDropSuspect` documents and asserts precisely this: "the single-peer (no `peer` label) exposition", read via `exp.Value(...)`. The test's comment ("a single-peer edge … exposes their one peer UNNAMED (peer='')") inverts the documented back-compat rule — `peer=""` label values exist only in the *multi-peer* exposition's unnamed primary. Consequence in the privileged run: the D36 precondition `Fatalf`s ("released_frames_total{peer=\"\"} absent … after traffic flowed") on a perfectly healthy system, and the core rebaselines/dropSuspect assertions would otherwise silently read 0/0. The test cannot pass as written.

**Finding 2 — unprivileged, the test FAILS instead of SKIPS (acceptance falsified operationally).** I ran `go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run TestOneSidedRestartRecovery` in this unprivileged environment: `requireNetAdmin`'s `ip link add … dummy` probe *succeeds* (TestMain's `unshare -Urmn` user namespace grants CAP_NET_ADMIN), then both subtests FAIL 5s later with `CreateTUN("wanbond0") failed; /dev/net/tun does not exist` — `--- FAIL … 11.86s`, not a skip. The acceptance requires "skips (not fails) without privileges" and the file's own doc comment (lines 57-59) claims the same; both are falsified. The gate must also probe `/dev/net/tun` (which the doc comment itself lists as a fixture requirement).

Minor: the post-restart `waitLink(tunDev, …)` checks (lines 177, 240) are vacuous under `tun_persist=true` — the link persists across the stop, so the check succeeds regardless of re-adoption, yet its failure message claims "not re-adopted after restart". The subsequent session/ping assertions carry the real load; fix the check or its message.
